original_hypothesis.title: LockingMultiRewards processExpiredLocks is operator-only with no user self-service fallback
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: processExpiredLocks is the ONLY mechanism to convert expired locked balances to unlocked balances, and it requires onlyOperators access. There is no function allowing users to unlock their own expired locks. If all operators become unavailable (lost keys, deregistered, or malicious refusal), users' staked tokens are permanently locked with no recovery path.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The project documentation confirms reliance on Gelato automation for lock processing. A single point of failure in the off-chain automation permanently freezes user funds. The contract has no emergency unlock or user-initiated unlock path.
deployment_assumptions: At least one operator (Gelato) must remain operational and willing to process locks
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: LockingMultiRewards.s.sol: ownership transferred to safe.ops; operators not set in deploy script (must be set post-deployment). Documentation states Gelato is used.

proof_of_signal.pattern: onlyOperators modifier on processExpiredLocks; no alternative user-callable unlock function exists in contract

preconditions: All operators become unavailable (key loss, deregistration, or malicious refusal)
Users have locked tokens with expired unlock times

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: onlyOperators with no user fallback; no emergency unlock; no timelock-based auto-unlock
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Single point of failure in off-chain operator infrastructure permanently locks user funds
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No user self-service unlock; no emergency owner-bypass; no time-based auto-unlock

analysis_questions: Can the owner add themselves as an operator to restore functionality?
Is there a governance timelock before operator changes take effect?
Could the owner pause then unpause to trigger some unlock mechanism?
validation_requirements: Confirm owner can call setOperator to add new operators
Verify there is a path for owner to restore lock processing if operators go offline
Test that withdraw() cannot bypass locked balance (it can't - it only uses unlocked)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: The Gelato automation service experiences an outage or the operator keys are compromised/lost. Users with expired locks cannot convert them to unlocked balances. Their tokens remain in locked state indefinitely. While the owner could set a new operator, there may be significant delay. During this time, users cannot access their locked funds.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Temporary to permanent lock of all user locked tokens
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Owner can add new operator via setOperator; requires owner key availability

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for lock expiry without operator processing

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Observable when processExpiredLocks calls stop occurring

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
